Title: Rail & Ramble: A bit of rough up Top Withens
Summary:
Join us for a walk to mark 210 years since the birth of Charlotte Brontë, born on 21 April 1816. As we explore the moors, reservoirs and valleys around Haworth, we’ll be walking through the very landscapes that shaped her life and her imagination.
We’ll take a ride out on the heritage Keighley & Worth Valley Railway service from Keighley to the charming village of Oxenhope to start our walk. We'll amble steadily onto the moors before skirting the edge of Lower Laithe Reservoir, a lovely stretch of water framed by rolling hills. From here we'll continue towards Ponden Reservoir, enjoying open views as the landscape becomes wilder and more dramatic.
The climb up to
Top Withens rewards us with panoramas across good old Brontë country! This area is often misquoted as the inspiration for Emily Brontë's
'Wuthering Heights'
. After taking in more beautiful views, we'll descend on a moorland path to the Brontë Waterfall and Bridge, a picture‑perfect place to pause before the final leg.
The route finishes with a gentle return into Haworth, where cafés and cobbled streets await. We'll then catch the steam train back to Keighley.
Dogs:
Well-behaved, socialised and under-control dogs are welcome on this event.
Dogs should be kept on a lead whenever this is signposted, whenever we are in the vicinity of livestock, and at the event leader's request. If it's hot, you'll need to make sure you're carrying sufficient extra water. If you're unsure, contact the leader for guidance.
If the event is fully booked:
Please
join the waiting list as people frequently drop out during the week before an event and you are likely to get a place on the walk.
If this is your first OutdoorLads walk event:
If this is your first event, we’ll make sure you feel welcomed. Please
check out the My First Event page
, and don’t hesitate to drop me a message if you have questions or want to know more about this walk.
Image / photo credits:
All leader's own images - with permission for OutdoorLads to use.
Difficulty & Distance: Total distance about 17 km (approx. 10.5 miles) with 500m (1640 feet) ascent in stages. Approximately 5 hours including lunch. Some areas of moorland will be boggy even in dry conditions.

Title: Hike in the Howardian Hills
Summary:
Join Lee on a 12km (7.5 mile) hike in the heart of the picturesque Howardian Hills in North Yorkshire. The route will take us along the Fryton Millenium Trail and up onto a ridge, which will give us great views of the North Yorkshire countryside.
If it is open when we pass, we may have the opportunity (if the group wishes) to stop off at
The Grapes Inn
in the village of
Slingsby
, for a quick beverage before continuing the walk. The pub is dog friendly according to their site, indeed "friendly to any 4-legged animal" ...just leave your goats and sheep at home please ;).
Well-behaved dogs are welcome on this walk; however, please note we will be going through some fields containing horses so they will need to be kept on a lead a certain parts of the route.
Although we won't be walking nearby,
Yorkshire Lavender
is a short drive away for those wanting to include this on their visit.
If the event is fully booked:
please join the waiting list as people frequently drop out during the week before an event and you are likely to get a place on the walk.
Your First OutdoorLads Event?
We know that new members in particular like to know in advance more about what an OutdoorLads event will be like.
If this is your first OutdoorLads event, you should check out the information designed specifically for you on the
My First Event
page on the OutdoorLads website.
Image credits: all images provided by event Leader. OutdoorLads has permission to use them.
Difficulty & Distance: The 12km (7.5 mile) route is mainly on mud and gravel tracks. The first quarter of the walk is very flat with the whole walk being in total 130m of ascent (gentle slopes).

Title: Sunday jaunt over Blackstone Edge (circular)
Summary:
7.5 miles (12km) exposed circular walk on top of the hills (approx. 4.5 hours)
A Sunday afternoon circular upland walk across the Grasslands and stones of Blackstone Edge. These are the hills you see as you drive past Junction 22 of the M62. Rolling grassland scarred by fishers and fires. The walk will start close to Junction 22 (for ease of access) and head into and over the hills towards top of Cragg Vale. The route takes in views over Rochdale, Oldham, Manchester and beyond.
This is a very exposed walk. There are no shelters, trees or woodlands on route to shelter in - so if it's sunny and hot expect it to be very sunny and hot, and if it's wet and windy expect it to be very wet and very windy (although we do walk past the White House pub on the way around).
Please bring appropriate clothing and footwear.
Dogs:
Well-behaved, socialised and under-control dogs are welcome on this events. Dogs must be kept on a lead whenever we are around livestock, or when requested by the event leader.
Waiting List:
If this event is fully booked and you would like to attend, please add yourself to the waiting list. Places often become available closer to the date, and it is likely you will still get a place.
New Members:
If you are thinking of making this your first event with ODL and have any concerns or questions, please don't hesitate to drop me a message, or go to our
'My First Event
' page.
Start times:
We will aim to start on time, but appreciate different people have different needs. To accommodate this, I will aim to be at the meeting point 20 minutes before the start time (I will be wearing a bright red Outdoor Lads T-shirt). We will wait up-to 10 minutes after the start time for people to arrive before setting off.
Route mapped on OS Maps App, available from: BlobFish85
Photo: All taken by event leader
Difficulty & Distance: Upland walk on narrow, uneven and eroded paths - can be difficult underfoot in areas.

Title: Osmotherly Day Walk - North Yorkshire Moors
Summary:
Join Lee on one of his regular ODL walks that takes in great views around the picturesque village of Osmotherley, with lake, river, forest, and moorland vistas. This includes views across to the Yorkshire Dales and Roseberry Topping, Cod Beck Reservoir, as well as the village itself! There may also be the chance of a post-walk ice cream in the car park and/or visit to one of the village pubs or cafe.
Well behaved dogs welcome one this walk.
If the event is fully booked:

Title: The Blue-Green Trail
Summary:
Join Andy and the Outdoor Lads for a sensational springtime
Sunday
stroll around Sale and Chorlton Water Parks.
Join us for a relaxing day out on a refreshing and scenic journey that blends gentle activity with beautiful natural surroundings. Beginning near Sale Water Park, we will pass the lively marina, along the peaceful edge of the lake, where open water views and passing wildlife create a calm, uplifting atmosphere right from the start.
As we continue, the route joins the River Mersey, following its winding course through green corridors and tranquil stretches that feel far removed from the bustle of the city. It’s an easygoing walk designed to be enjoyed at a relaxed pace—perfect for conversation, fresh air, and taking in the scenery.
Our journey will lead us to Chorlton Water Park, a serene and picturesque spot where we’ll pause to enjoy lunch. Surrounded by woodland and a peaceful lake, it’s an ideal place to unwind, recharge, and soak up the natural beauty of the area.
The walk is approximately 8 miles in total, with a flat route and good-quality paths throughout, making it accessible and comfortable for most walkers.
Whether you’re looking to connect with nature, enjoy some light exercise, or simply spend time in good company, this walk offers a welcoming and enjoyable experience from start to finish.
This is a relaxed walk. Approximately 8 miles. Flat. Good quality paving.
TRANSPORT:
The tram stop for
Sale Water Park
will drop you off at the bottom of Rifle Road, and the Tree Tops Cafe is approximately 5 mins. walk from there.
Please do not get the tram to Sale !
The Metrolink can be boarded at Market Street in Manchester city centre, (at the top end near Piccadilly Gardens outside Primark).
Plenty of free parking available. Several electric charging points also available.
Well-behaved, socialised and under-control dogs are welcome on this event.
Dogs must be kept on a lead whenever this is signposted and whenever we are in the vicinity of livestock, and at the event leader's request. It's a good idea to check what the route may be like with the event leader before booking to find out more about the potential suitability of the walk for dogs.

Title: A Ramble by the Channel, and a Traversal to a Castle
Summary:
This walk is a staple of the OutdoorLads schedule, but this version includes a ferry trip from the harbourside hamlet of Keyhaven to the sea-guarding stronghold of Hurst Castle. The boat ride will give great views of the multitudinous marsh of the harbour and the precarious mass of the castle, precarious because Henry VIII's and Victoria's great guardian of The Solent has just been stopped from collapsing into the sea. The Henrician part is safe and open to visitors, but the Victorian part is unsafe and closed while it is rescued by English Heritage. The attractive town of Lymington is at the start and end of this walk where the New Forest meets the sea and we gaze out over it to the Isle of Wight.
The sights:
Lymington:
The New Forest visitor website states:
'
The historic town of Lymington offers plenty for the visitor to the New Forest. This colourful coastal town is an ancient seaport with a rich maritime history. Situated on the west bank of the Lymington River, on the edge of the Solent, Lymington is a major yachting centre with three marinas...  From the Middle Ages and up to the 19th century, Lymington was well-known for its salt making and from the early 19th century a thriving ship building industry. Much of the architecture you see today in Lymington is Georgian and Victorian. The High Street, which looks predominantly Georgian, is in fact made up of an eclectic mix of Georgian, Victorian, Art Deco with some traces of Medieval architecture.'
Keyhaven:
A pretty hamlet with a harbour beside Keyhaven Marshes and behind Hurst Spit. There are no shops, but some attractive cottages and a pub.
Lymington and Keyhaven Marshes:
Forming in the lee of Hurst Spit, the combined area of the two nature reserves covers over 1200 hectares. It includes mudflats, salt marshes, shingle banks, coastal grazing marshes, and saline lagoons. The nature reserves support important populations of birds and rare and specialist plants and invertebrates. Salt harvesting was a source of income for local people from Roman times to 1865 when the last salt pan closed. For information click the link
here
. The ferry goes from Keyhaven across the harbour to Hurst Castle continuously. The fare is £6. If there are many of us, we'll have to cross in shifts.
Hurst Castle:
English Heritage website states: 'Built by Henry VIII...it was one of the most advanced artillery fortresses in England. Commanding the narrow entrance to the Solent, it offers stunning panoramic views. The castle was used as a prison for eminent 17th century captives, including Charles I. It was later strengthened during the 19th and 20th centuries and played a role in defending the western Solent from invasion threats from the Napoleonic Wars to the Second World War...In February 2021, a section of wall on the 19th-century east wing of the castle collapsed, after the sea exposed and undercut its foundations'. New rock armour has been laid to protect the castle and the structure has been secured but not restored. For more information click the link
here
. If you would like to go in the castle, it costs £9.50.
Hurst Beach and Spit:
A mighty bank of shingle that half-closes off The Solent to the English Channel. Naturally formed by longshore drift but now requiring the regular artificial replenishment of its material to avoid being breached by the sea. A breach would be disastrous for the nature reserves behind the spit and for the commercial shipping and leisure boating in The Solent.
The
route
:
We'll head south from Lymington town centre to the Flushards area. We'll then follow lanes heading southwest to Normandy across the boatyards which is where we'll pick up the Solent Way to take us across Pennington and Keyhaven Marshes. At the jetty for the ferry we'll embark on the two kilometre trip to Hurst Castle. After lunch and a possible look at the castle we'll walk along Hurst Spit and have a swim and ice creams at Hurst Beach. The return journey will entail walking along the harbourside at Keyhaven and then along a byway through Pennington and then a lane through Woodside which is a suburb of Lymington. A route north will bring us to the High Street in Lymington which we'll walk down to end up at the quayside for a drink or the station for home.
Dogs:
I love having dogs on my walks and this walk is suitable for them apart from during very hot weather. There is sea for them to splash in much of the way but little shade. A dog off the lead must be obedient.

You MUST complete a
Participation Statement
,
in addition
to booking your event space before attending an OutdoorLads event. You only need to complete this Participation Statement once, not for each event you attend.

Title: London Indoor Climbing Evening - All abilities- Beginners welcome
Summary:
Come join OutdoorLads for indoor climbing in North London!
At the biggest climbing centre in North London, there are 450 routes over 95 lines set across 1000 square metres of wall offering top-roping, lead climbing and bouldering on a wide range of slabs, cracks, overhangs, off-widths and chimneys - there really is something to suit every level of climbing ability!
I've never climbed before, can I come along? YES- but contact the Event Leader (see below)!
Supervised sessions
If you're unsure whether you need to be signed in as a supervised climber or not, if you answer
NO
to any of the following three questions, then you will need to arrange to be supervised. If you can answer YES to all these questions, you can sign up to this event as an unsupervised climber:
1. Can you fit a climbing harness correctly?
2. Can you tie yourself to the end of a rope using the correct knots?
3. Can you belay another climber correctly and confidently?
To come along, you will need to register via this event page, and to climb you will need to pay an entry fee directly to the centre when you arrive.
See below.
Also to make climbing more accessible, the Castle has kindly provided us with some group benefits:
If you are climbing with the group, tell the reception when paying for entry and you will get the concession rate. That's £12.50 instead of £18 for full price entry!
For those in need of financial support, we have access to some free entries. Speak to the Event Leader about how this works. We need to provide to the Castle some basic details (name, DoB, email) 48 hours before the session.
Tokens are available for free shoe or harness hire. We have two ODL harnesses that we can lend beginners climbing with the group- so get the free hire shoes!
NOTE: If you are climbing with us for the first time,
either as an experienced climber or beginner,
please send a note to the Event Leader
telling them about your experience and goals.
There is a limit to the number of beginners that we can accommodate at each session. Each beginner needs to be supervised so we  need to ensure there are enough experienced climbings to help you out. Climbing is great fun! It's a fab workout, in a social environment. Give it a go!
Can I just come along and boulder?
Absolutely! Most of us do roped climbing on these Tuesdays. If you prefer bouldering, you can sign yourself in and do it, as long as you can fill in the climbing centre's form correctly, watch their safety video and can answer the simple safety questions at reception on your first visit. Meet the group at the start; some others might be bouldering. And definitely come join us afterwards in the pub.
Participation Statement
IMPORTANT!
Please note that every person participating should be aware of (and accept) that those members perceived as ‘in charge’ or leading the event are not necessarily qualified, do not need to be qualified, and are not regarded by OutdoorLads, or themselves as ‘technical experts’ but are amateur climbers with some experience and who are happy to impart their knowledge. Any advice given should be considered with this in mind by the recipient.
All those attending the event must also be aware of the centre's own terms and conditions of participation and abide by them, along with any instructions given by the centre’s staff. Everyone must also take responsibility for their own safety while participating in Indoor climbing events.
Anyone with COVID-19 symptoms, even if they are extremely mild, or who has recently tested positive for Covid-19, is asked not to attend.
Can't make this event?
We run weekly climbing and bouldering events in the South East, so why not check out what's coming up near you here:
http://outdoorlads.events/SouthEastIndoorClimbing
. Alternatively, stay updated on upcoming events using the social media channels below:
Telegram
(Climbing and bouldering only):
OutdoorLads (SE) Climbing
Facebook:
http://fb.com/OutdoorLadsSouthEast
Twitter:
@OdlSouthEast
Difficulty & Distance: With top-rope and lead climbs at every grade from complete beginner to seasoned pro, you decide how hard it gets!

Title: Bouldering MANCHESTER - Beginners welcome
Summary:
Join OutdoorLads For An Evening of INDOOR BOULDERING in Manchester!
This event is intended as a safe space for the LGBTQ+ community; everyone will be warmly welcomed
Bouldering is a form of climbing that focuses on shorter "problems" emphasising technique, balance, body positioning, and sometimes power, rather than long, high routes. Without the need for ropes, harnesses, and belaying, bouldering is an accessible activity for newcomers and is often considered the "purest" form of climbing.
Bouldering offers a wide range of benefits, including:
Physical:
Improved strength, tone, balance, power, and coordination.
Mental:
Enhanced problem-solving, focus, concentration, and body awareness.
Emotional:
Relaxation, a sense of achievement, and a feeling of mind-body connection.
Bouldering is also a fantastic way to meet new people and build lasting friendships, as it's an inherently social sport where climbers share tips and strategies to conquer the routes.
New to Bouldering?
If you're new to bouldering, it is recommended that you have a guided session from the event leader to learn the basics of safe bouldering. The leader can then introduce you to the rest of the group where you can refine your newly learnt skills in a social environment.
Experienced Boulderer?
If you’re new to climbing with ODL, the leader will help to introduce you to the rest of the group so you can explore the centre together.
You are responsible for your own safety and actions while bouldering. Please use your own judgement when accepting advice from OutdoorLads Volunteers or other experienced climbers, regardless of your bouldering experience.
Ready to boulder?
You will need to sign up on the ODL event page. We would ask you to do this each time so that the leader has access to important information
If you are not already registered with Rock Over Climbing, please do so
here
to save time when you arrive. You only need to do this once.
When you arrive at the centre you will need to pay for entry and hire climbing shoes if you don't own a pair. ODL members benefit from the concessionary rate so please mention this when paying.
Get bouldering! You’ll find most of the group already bouldering so feel free to join them. If you’re new to bouldering with ODL, the leader will be looking out for you so if you spot them, please go speak with them first.
Any questions?
If you have any questions, please contact the leader directly.
Photos : Copyright granted to OutdoorLads by
Sieh
and joenellist.
Difficulty & Distance: Suitable for all abilities. If you're new to bouldering, just let the event leader know and we'll get you started!

Title: Indoor Climbing MANCHESTER - Beginners welcome!
Summary:
This event is intended as a safe space for the LGBTQ+ community; everyone will be warmly welcomed
Join us for an evening of climbing in one of the UK's top climbing centres. Whether you are new to climbing or a seasoned climber, this centre offers something for everyone with a huge selection of routes including top rope, lead and auto belay.
Prefer to boulder? That's well catered for as well, with an extensive modern bouldering area ensuring there is plenty to keep you busy, or you can swap between climbing and bouldering on the same night.
Climbing is the main focus of the event, but this is also a great chance to meet other members and build lasting friendships. Many Outdoor Lads members join through climbing, so if you're new to the group and want to know more, come along and chat over a cuppa during the tea and cake break.
What to expect on your first climb with us?
If you are an experienced climber and have passed the centre's proficiency test, we'll pair you up with a climbing buddy and you'll be out exploring the centre in no time.
If you have never climbed before or think you could do with a refresher, we offer supervised climbing sessions with one of our leaders, subject to availability. That session will focus on introducing you to climbing, making sure you get plenty of time on the wall whilst also showing you how to belay safely. Due to the centre's rules, we are limited in the number of climbers the leader is able to supervise in each session so if you think you may need a supervised session,
please contact the leader before signing up to make sure there is enough space.
As a general guide, if you answer "no" to any of the following questions, you should opt for a supervised session:
Can you fit a climbing harness correctly?
Can you tie into a rope using the correct knots?
Can you belay and lower down another climber correctly and confidently?
After a few supervised sessions, you should be ready to complete the centre's proficiency test and once that has been passed, you'll be able to climb with the rest of the group.
Everything else you need to know
You will need to register with Big Depot before your first visit to the centre but you only need to do this once. If you are an experienced climber, you can save time and do this online
here
, selecting the 18+ unsupervised option. This also applies if you just want to boulder, just select the relevant options during the registration. If you are a beginner and require a supervised session, the leader will help register you when you are at the centre.
Photos provided by Sieh and Dexmonster.
Difficulty & Distance: There is a range of routes of varying difficulty, but there should be something to suit everybody

Title: BSC26: Snowdonia Waterfalls Circular (Sun)
Summary:
This is one of the events organised as part of
Big Spring Camp 2026
.
This is a moderate 18 km circular walk in Snowdonia (Eryri), starting and finishing at Big Spring Camp. The route links a series of natural features using established paths and woodland trails. From the campsite, the walk begins on forest tracks, heading into woodland. Our first stop is Grey Mare’s Tail Waterfall, a dramatic narrow waterfall plunging through a wooded rocky gorge. The route continues up hill on defined trails toward Llyn y Sarnau, a quiet upland lake; this is a relatively level section with open views across the water.
From the lake, the path returns to woodland and leads downhill to Ty Hyll, often called the Ugly House. The route then descends on well-used paths toward Swallow Falls, a powerful, multi-tiered waterfall on the River Llugwy, surrounded by woodland and rocky cascades. From Swallow Falls, the walk joins a section of the Snowdonia Slate Trail, following it into Betws-y-Coed where shops and cafés are available.
The return leg leaves Betws-y-Coed on forest tracks, heading back through forestry managed woodland with wide tracks and steady up and down gradients, before rejoining paths leading back to Big Spring Camp.
Photo credits - creative commons licence: TCEexplorer (Ugly House), David Dixon (Swallow Falls), Andy Stephenson (Grey Mare's Tail), David Dixon (Betws-y-Coed). All available from Geograph
www.geograph.org.uk
Difficulty & Distance: 600m height climb, 18km walk.

Title: BSC26: Marin MTB (Sun)
Summary:
This is one of the events organised as part of
Big Spring Camp 2026
.
Today's ride will be lead by our partners
OUTMTB
, so if you're on
Spond
please sign up there and leave this event page for the others.
After the inevitable kit faff that is involved with all mountain biking trips we will head up to Gwydir Mawr (Marin trail). on the Sunday morning.
This ride covers the gem that is the Gwydir Mawr (Marin trail). With its beautifully sculpted berms, table top jumps and natural sections, this ride really has it all. This is a 25 km ride on predominantly red graded trails with several extensive climbs and some awe-inspiring descents that will leave you breathless. Literally. This makes it a very technically and physically demanding ride for experienced mountain bikers with good stamina on mountain bikes
Please note this ride is only suitable for experienced MTB riders
on good mountain bikes. (Not suitable for beginners or hybrid bikes, sorry!)
Photos:
Taken by event leaders and used by OutdoorLads with permission.
Difficulty & Distance: 25 km - 5 hours

Title: BSC26: Aber Falls Circular and Whisky Wind-down (Sun)
Summary:
This is one of the events organised as part of
Big Spring Camp 2026
.
Join Mike for a 7 mile walk starting Morfa Aber Bird sanctuary surrounded by salt marshes, hopefully we'll spot an oyster catcher or  two. Then we'll follow the coastal path heading inland towards Abergwyngregyn gently climbing up alongside the Afon Rhaeadr-fawr river to the spectacular Rhaedr Fawr / Aber Falls one of the most beautifu waterfalls in Wales. We'll also check out the other cascades nearby including Rhaedr-Bach which is surrounded by iron age remains and panoramic views of the valley and the sea.
Then we'll head back to the village and onto visit Aber Falls Distillery Visitor centre which offers gin and whisky tours & tastings; and have a "
diferyn bach
" (Welsh for
wee dram).
All photos taken by Mike although these pictures are not of Aber Falls, but look similar!!
Difficulty & Distance: Lowland walk to the waterfall

Title: BSC26: Llyn Crafnant (Sun)
Summary:
This is one of the events organised as part of
Big Spring Camp 2026
.
A moderate, scenic day walk leaving right from the campsite passing through varied terrain - lakeside, forest and rocky paths.
We'll walk from the campsite up through the forest on steep paths and drop in on Llanrhychwyn chapel. Passing by the edge of Lake Geirionydd we'll head up to Llyn Crafnant . After a lunch stop (bring a packed lunch) we will circle round descending through ancient woodlands with views of Snowdonia at Nant Uchaf. Finally we will pass the Gray Mares Tail waterfall before reaching camp.
Image Credits: copyrightfreephotos.com; Andy Renault & Jonathan Brookes with permission granted to OutdoorLads for further use.
IMPORTANT! - Participation Statement
You MUST complete a
Participation Statement
,
in addition
to booking your event space before attending an OutdoorLads event. You only need to complete this Participation Statement once, not for each event you attend.
Difficulty & Distance: Approx 15 km with 300m of fairly steep ascent at the beginning, mostly good paths.

Title: BSC26: Cycling Along the North Wales coast, Bangor and the Las Ogwen Cycleway (Sun)
Summary:
This is one of the events organised as part of
Big Spring Camp 2026
.
Join us on a circular ride along the stunning North Wales coastline, Bangor and the Lon Las Ogwen cycle path.
This event is suitable for intermediate/experienced cyclists as we will be cycling at a steady pace for approximately 50 miles (80km). The route is hilly - this is Wales 🏴󠁧󠁢󠁷󠁬󠁳󠁿 - (about 1750 of climb) but we will have plenty of rest stops and we will not leave anyone behind.
Route information
We head north from the campsite to Conwy and then west along the stunning, vast, sand and skyline of the North Wales coast.
We will leave the coast just shy of Bangor - a quick trip to the old cathedral city is also an option! Then we will head inland in the direction of Lon Las Ogwen and the first section of National Cycle Network Route 82, which runs from Bangor, entering the Snowdonia National Park at Bethesda.
It largely follows the route of the old railway line that carried slate down the valley from the Bethesda slate quarry to the coast where it was shipped around the world. The route is listed in the Guardian's "
Five of the best scenic bike rides in Wales
".
When we reach Llyn Ogwen, a beautiful ribbon-like lake, we'll cycle along the A5 between two of Snowdonia's mountain ranges, the Carneddau and the Glyderau. We will then continue on the A5 alongside the river to Capel Currig before turning off the main road at Pont-Cyfyng and onwards to the final stretch of our cycle back to the campsite through the wooded foothills of Mynydd Bwlchyrhaearn and then a well-earned pint or two at the bar.
Note: I've tried to avoid too many A-roads, but a short section is unavoidable - most of it is on quiet, scenic roads.
Click here for an overview of the cycle ride:
https://explore.osmaps.com/route/21178229/odl-cycle-north-wales-coastline-bangor-and-las-ogwen
This trip is suitable for
road or hybrid bikes
.
Photos taken by event leader.
IMPORTANT! - Participation Statement
You
MUST
complete the
Cycling Participation Statement
in addition
to booking your event space
before
attending a cycling event.
Difficulty & Distance: Distance 50 miles (80km). The route is hilly - this is Wales - (about 1750m of climb)

Title: BSC26: Industry, Mythology and Ice Cream Walk (Sun)
Summary:
This is one of the events organised as part of
Big Spring Camp 2026
.
Join Will and OutdoorLads for a hiking tour into the history & legends of Cwm Bychan, through the leafy forests of the Aberglaslyn valley, navigating abandoned copper mine facilities nestled in a mountain pass, before treading carefully around Llyn Dinas and the ancient ruins of the Dinas Emrys castle, both steeped in history & Arthurian legend. All topped off with an award winning ice cream at the end.
Starting off in
Beddgelert
, a charming Snowdonia village at the confluence of two rivers to form the River Glaslyn and home of the legend of Prince Llewelyn’s dog, Gelert. After parking in the main car park, we will walk through to the riverside and past
Gelert’s grave
. Then crossing the Welsh Highland Railway track, proceed riverside through the
Aberglaslyn Pass
along the narrow but picturesque
Fisherman’s Walk
to Pont Aberglaslyn. Following the path through the
Nantmor forest
and the ascent out into
Cwm Bychan
, navigating the abandoned mechanical remains of the
17th Century copper mine
facilities. At the summit of the Cwm, we will descend down to the lake
Llyn Dinas
, the site of an Arthurian mythological battle; walking along its banks to start a second ascent, weather and time dependent, partly via the
Watkin Path,
stopping at the ancient ruins of
Dinas Emrys
castle, a relic of Iron Age history and Arthurian legend that was said to be built upon a giant dragon's tomb. We will then cross
Merlin's Pool
& waterfall before following the River Gwynant past the
Sygun Copper Mine
back to Beddgelert for some refreshments and amazing ice cream can be found (highly recommend the strawberry cheesecake flavour!)
This event is a circular walk, meeting at the BSC campsite first at 8.30am before driving to the nearby village of Beddgelert; carpooling available. It will be a full day so you’ll need a packed lunch, some snacks and plenty of fluid to drink. Walking boots are essential, as the ground is rough in places and may be wet and muddy, depending on the weather.
Note that this is not a stroll in the park: it’s a serious 18km walk with 2 ascents as we climb through the Cwm Bychan valley (ascent is approx 400 meters) - you will need a decent level of fitness. On the day, I may need to shorten the hike if the weather is not ideal, but it will still be a great trip. If you want to take a look at the route, it is available on
OS Maps.
Approx Itinerary
8.30 - meet at BSC assembly point, leave for Beddgelert 8.40.
9:15 - arrive at Beddgelert.
13:30 - stop for lunch.
15:00/16:00 - Arrival back at Beddgelert for ice cream, maybe a beer/beverage, then back to camp
Your First OutdoorLads Walk Event?
We know that new members in particular like to know in advance more about what an OutdoorLads event will be like.
If this is your first OutdoorLads event, you should check out the information designed specifically for you on the My First Event page on the OutdoorLads website. But please do not hesitate and contact Will if you have any questions.
Photo Credits: Taken by Tom Benians
IMPORTANT! - Participation Statement
You MUST complete a
Participation Statement
,
in addition
to booking your event space before attending an OutdoorLads event. You only need to complete this Participation Statement once, not for each event you attend.
Difficulty & Distance: This is a 18km hike with 2 ascents and up to 5-6 hours of trekking. It is not for the faint hearted or those with low levels of fitness.

Title: BSC26: Yr Wyddfa/Snowdon (Sun)
Summary:
This is one of the events organised as part of
Big Spring Camp 2026
.
Join Neil for a walk up
Yr Wyddfa (Snowdon)
by one of the least well known paths.  We will either ascend via the South Ridge or the Rhyd Ddu path depending on weather conditions and the leader's chance for a recce before the event! :)
Please note that if you are really scared of heights this probably isn't one for you
- the ridges are walkable and aren't anything like Crib Goch (crikey, Crib Goch scares me!) but do have steep drops either side at points which you do need to be aware of.  Feel free to message me (or watch one of the many videos of the route on YouTube to get an idea) if unsure.
While Yr Wyddfa is seen as a "touristy" mountain by many, it's still the highest mountain in Wales at 1085m, and so there's plenty of climb and the weather could throw anything at us, so make sure you're properly kitted out as below - this isn't one for jeans and Converse!
Apologies for the really early start on this one but parking for Snowdon on a bank holiday can be an absolute nightmare so we need to go early to ensure we can park as there are very limited options.
Waiting list
It's likely this event will get full very quickly but people do change their plans so even if there is a waiting list please book on anyway, this also gives us an idea of demand and there is a chance of a place if someone cancels.  Please don't however book onto this unless you are committed to actually attending it because there is very high demand for Snowdon walks on Big Spring Camp and so if people book speculatively and don't actually attend on the day then some may miss out.
Weather conditions
We may need to change the walk or cancel it in the event that weather conditions would make it unsafe (particularly as this walk involves a ridge) - or if on the day we can't get parked as there's only one (reasonably sized) car park at Rhyd Ddu.  I will let you know as soon as I can if this looks likely.
Photo Credits

>, via Wikimedia Commons

>, via Wikimedia Commons

https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/2.0
>, via Wikimedia Commons

You MUST complete a
Participation Statement
,
in addition
to booking your event space before attending an OutdoorLads event. You only need to complete this Participation Statement once, not for each event you attend.
Difficulty & Distance: Yr Wyddfa is known as quite touristy but is still a proper mountain with some steep bits and drops and is a reasonably long day.  Approximately 12km, 887m ascent.

Title: BSC26: Chuck Chuck Baby Screening in Trefriw
Summary:
Why not start your Saturday evening off at
BIG Spring Camp
by joining the local film club in Trefriw Village Hall to watch a screening of the film "Chuck Chuck Baby"!
Synopsis: Helen lives with her ex-husband, his 20-year-old girlfriend, their new baby - and his dying mother Gwen. Her life is a grind, and like all the other women she toils with at the local chicken factory, is spent in service of the clock. She lives only for laughing with her friends at work, caring for Gwen, and music. When Joanne, the girl she secretly loved at school, comes back to town, Helen's world is turned upside down.
The local film club (
Trefriw Film Club
) have invited us to join them at Trefriw Village Hall on Saturday evening for the screening. This is just a short walk from our base at Clwb Rygbi Nant Conwy, in the next village. You're welcome to bring your own snacks and drinks, they also sell a small selection there.
Attendees are welcome to arrive from 7pm at the village hall, with the screening starting at 7:30pm.
You will need to pay £5 on the door upon arrival
. Cash or card accepted.
Image from Trefriw Film Club.

Title: BSC26: Conwy Mountain (Sat)
Summary:
This is one of the events organised as part of
Big Spring Camp 2026
.
Starting and finishing in the picturesque coastal town of Conwy with its imposing 13th century caste, this 10Km (6mile) loop gives stunning views for not too much effort, perfect for blowing the cobwebs away! There is a single short steep climb up Conwy Mountain where (weather permitting) we'll be treated to spectacular panoramic views. Following lunch at the summit, we will descend gradually through undulating countryside as we return to Conwy. The final section of the route follows the Conwy Estuary, with great views of the castle and the bridge. There will be time at the end of the walk to explore the town before our return to the campsite.
Total height gain 330m.
If you would like to attend this walk after completing the
Conway parkrun
you can meet us at the start of the walk in Conwy.
Please sign up on this event page and let the leader know that you will be joining us after the parkrun.
Photo credits: all images provided by the event leader. OutdoorLads has permission to use them.
Difficulty & Distance: One steep climb but nothing too arduous, we'll take our time!

Title: BSC26: Industrial Heritage Walk (Sat)
Summary:
This is one of the events organised as part of
Big Spring Camp 2026
.
Join us for a 12km circular walk from the rugby cub including a shed load of industrial heritage.
You will have time for parkrun beforehand if that way inclined ... or just have a hangover.
From the rugby club we bimble behind Trefriw to
Klondyke mill
, an impressive ruin.  Constructed in 1900, the mill was built to receive lead and zinc ore from Pandora mine, some 2 miles away.  The mill saw little usage; Pandora mine was never profitable after construction of the mill, and the mine ceased operation in 1905. Klondyke mill itself closed in 1911 after having a short succession of optimistic owners.
From there we cut through the woods to
Clogwyn y Fuwch Slate Quarry
, begun as early as the eighteenth century.  The site is unusual with workings on six levels going into a near-vertical scarp of some 400 feet on very steeply sloping ground. Sledges were originally used but later an incline brought material down to road level.  There are a couple of caves to look at (and enter at your own risk).
If time we will head to the
Hafna Lead Mine
.  The Hafna Mine has the most extensive and best preserved remains of any of the mines on the Gwydyr estate and it was once one of the most important lead mining areas in the United Kingdom.
This walk is rated hard as there is a 200m steep scramble, which you will curse and hate the leader for, but the views and industrial features are worth the slog.
Event full?
Please join the waiting list.  Places usually open up as the event gets nearer and, where necessary, we try to add additional leaders to increase the numbers, so you are highly likely to get a place.
Image
/
photo
credits
:  All images (c) Will Allaway with permission for OutdoorLads to use and reuse.
Difficulty & Distance: There is a short section of steep climbing/walking.

Title: BSC26: Twin Waters Walk (Sat)
Summary:
This is one of the events organised as part of
Big Spring Camp 2026
.
An easy-going, scenic day walk from the campsite with a lakeside lunch stop and a visit to a second lake on the way back.
We'll walk from the campsite up through the forest to get magnificent views of the hills of Snowdonia, visit an 11th Century chapel right out in the fields and meander along the edge of Llyn Geirionydd. We will stop here for lunch (there are toilets here but no shop). We will head over to our second lake, Llyn Crafnant, before a gradual descent to the village of Trefriw, where we will make a pitstop at the 'Fairy Falls' before heading back to camp.
Note: the 'Llyn Crafnant' walk on Sunday is very similar to this one, so you won't probably want to do both.
Image Credits: copyrightfreephotos.com; Andy Renault with permission granted to OutdoorLads for further use.
Difficulty & Distance: 12km with 300m of fairly steep ascent at the beginning, mostly good paths.

Title: BSC26: Coed-y-Brenin MTB (Sat)
Summary:
This is one of the events organised as part of
Big Spring Camp 2026
.
Today's ride will be lead by our partners
OUTMTB
, so if you're on
Spond
please sign up there and leave this event page for the others.
Join
OUTMTB
for this ride at the UK’s first (and still one of the best) mountain biking trail centres.
After the inevitable kit faff that is involved with all mountain biking trips we will head up to Coed Y Brenin on the Sunday morning and the plan is to ride the best bits of the red and black trails
Please note this ride is only suitable for experienced MTB riders
on good mountain bikes. (Not suitable for beginners or hybrid bikes, sorry!)
Photos:
Taken by event leaders and used by OutdoorLads with permission.

Title: BSC26: Two Lakes and a Waterfall (Sat)
Summary:
This is one of the events organised as part of
Big Spring Camp 2026
.
Starting at Nant Peris, the walk follows the south-west edge of Llyn Peris to our first stop, Dolbadarn Castle, a 13th-century fortress. From there, we continue to the spectacular Ceunant Mawr Falls and then we'll head into Llanberis, where you can pick up a few bits, before enjoying an early picnic lunch by the shores of Llyn Padarn. We then follow the scenic, oak-fringed Lôn Las Peris, passing water lily ponds, and head on to the westernmost tip of the lakes at Pont Penllyn.
Then we begin a steady climb up country lanes on the north side of the lakes towards Dinorwig, where excellent views open out across the wider mountainous landscape. From here it's onwards to the Dinorwig Slate Quarry on the slopes of Elidir Fawr, taking in the striking Sinc California and Vivian Quarry. We will then carefully descend via the zig-zag slate paths towards the Vaynol Arms Pub for a well-deserved pint.
The walk is moderate, undulating with a few small climbs - this is Wales 🏴󠁧󠁢󠁷󠁬󠁳󠁿 - totalling about 450 m. The walk is approximately 19 km in length.
OS Maps link to walk:
https://explore.osmaps.com/route/31171982/bsc26twolakesawaterfallnantperisllanberis
I can send you a GPX file, just drop us a line.
After meeting at BSC to arrange car shares, the walk will begin from the Park & Ride car park at Nant Paris .
This activity is also suitable for anyone taking part in the Conwy Parkrun (or up a bit late)!
Photos taken by event leader and are free to use for OutdoorLads.
IMPORTANT! - Participation Statement
You MUST complete a
Participation Statement
,
in addition
to booking your event space before attending an OutdoorLads event. You only need to complete this Participation Statement once, not for each event you attend.
Difficulty & Distance: There are a few long hills - this is Wales 🏴󠁧󠁢󠁷󠁬󠁳󠁿 - and the steep descent through the slate quarry can be slippy underfoot.

Title: BSC26: Llandudno and the Great Orme (Sat)
Summary:
This is one of the events organised as part of
Big Spring Camp 2026
.
An 8 mile walk along Conwy Sands and The North Wales Coastal Path, exploring Great Ormes Head and visiting Victorian Llandudno. This walk involves a short drive to the start.
This is an easy going walk combined with some light hearted tourism and is a contrast from the seriousness of the mountains.
Expect some fantastic coastal views looking back towards Snowdonia and out towards The Isle of Man. We start off along the shore line overlooking the vast expanse of Conwy Sands and Conwy Bay.
We will then go around the circumference and climb up onto the summit of The Great Orme - a headland jutting out into The Irish Sea. The geology, wildlife, archaeology and landscape of the Great Orme (Pen-y-Gogarth) on the Creuddyn Peninsula is of such importance that much of the headland has been designated a Special Area of Conservation, a Site of Special Scientific Interest and a Heritage Coast.
We will visit the Visitor Centre where you can buy refreshments and souvenirs. We will see the Victorian Electric Tramway and possibly the wild Kashmiri Goats. We will make our way to the famous Victorian resort of Llandudno passing the ski slope, Happy Valley and the pier.
There will be an opportunity to buy Fish and Chips or possibly get a drink before heading back to the camp site.
All photos taken by Andrew.
IMPORTANT! - Participation Statement
You MUST complete a
Participation Statement
,
in addition
to booking your event space before attending an OutdoorLads event. You only need to complete this Participation Statement once, not for each event you attend.
Difficulty & Distance: an easy 8 mile walk with a few steep bits

Title: BSC26:  Moel Siabod Hike (Sat)
Summary:
This is one of the events organised as part of
Big Spring Camp 2026
.
At 872m
Moel Siabod
just loses out on a place among the Welsh 3000's. However, its prominent position, standing away from the other main Snowdonia ranges, nevertheless gives it the feel of a "
proper mountain
". If you were going to draw a mountain you may well draw Moel Siabod!
Depending on the weather and the desires of the group we have a couple of options for our ascent. We can begin through a forest, or we could take a direct path straight up via deserted quarry buildings. Either way, we will ultimately find ourselves at the foot of an
impressive ridge
which offers walking or scrambling (where you need to use your hands) to the summit. Don't let the idea of
scrambling
put you off - this is a great place to try it for the first time as a few steps to the left will immediately find yourself back on two feet! that said, if it is wet or icy we will, of course, take the safer, walking option.
From the summit (where, if the weather is kind, the views are truly superb) we will descend the gentle slope toward Plas y Brenin.
***All photos taken by Mark House and used with permission***
Difficulty & Distance: Some scrambling where you need to use your hands, some bog, some steepness.  Around 10km.  Approx 800 metres of ascent

Title: BSC26: The High Carneddau & Aber Falls (Sat)
Summary:
This is one of the events organised as part of
Big Spring Camp 2026
.
Experience the wilder side of Eryri!
While many visitors flock to the busy paths of Yr Wyddfa, we’ll be heading to the northern reaches of the Carneddau—a vast, undulating plateau that offers some of the best "big mountain" feelings in Wales.
Our journey begins near the village of Abergwyngregyn. We’ll start with a steady climb that grants us a spectacular bird's-eye view of the famous
Rhaeadr-fawr (Aber Falls)
. From there, we leave the crowds behind as we head into the high country.
The Route Highlights:
The Big Peaks:
We’ll be bagging several summits, including
Llwytmor
, the massive
Foel-fras
(942m), and the beautifully named
Carnedd Gwenllian
.
The Views:
On a clear day, you’ll see across the Menai Strait to Anglesey and out toward the Irish Sea.
The Descent:
We’ll return via
Drum
and the shores of
Llyn Anafon
, following a historic mountain track that leads us gently back down to the valley.
This is a
strenuous mountain walk
covering approximately 15-17km with significant elevation gain. It’s perfect for those looking to stretch their legs, enjoy some remote wilderness, and chat with fellow OutdoorLads in a stunning setting. Whether this is your first BSC or your tenth, the Carneddau never fails to impress.
Photo Credits:
All photos taken by OutdoorLads members and permission is granted to OutdoorLads for further use.
Difficulty & Distance: Approximately 11 miles with an elevation of over 3000 ft during the day.

Title: BSC26: Quarry Karts (Sun PM)
Summary:
Please complete this waiver prior to the event.
This is one of the events organised as part of
BIG Spring Camp 2026
.
Quarry Karts is the UK's only mountain cart experience! This is a gravity-fuelled adrenaline rush on a three-wheeled cart. Avoid the obstacles on your way down the 3km slate track! Think you can beat your first time? You'll have a second attempt to set a new PB! If you love go-karting, this takes it to the next level!
The unique carts have a low centre of gravity and a widewheel base, so they're super stable. And they have independent hydraulic disc brakes to allow you to slide and drift around corners.
HOW MANY RUNS?
2
DURATION PER RUN
Approximately 5 mins per run
FULL EXPERIENCE DURATION
2 hours
TRACK LENGTH?
3km
OBSTACLES
Chicanes, banked corners, tunnels & more!
Restrictions
Weight: Maximum 130kg
Height: Minimum 1.2m
If you have
any medical conditions
, please see
check here
to ensure you're eligible to attend this event. You can contact
accessibility@zipworld.co.uk
prior to attending if you have any concerns.
Photos provided by and used with permission from Zip World.

Title: BSC26: Conwy parkrun (Sat)
Summary:
This is one of the events organised as part of
Big Spring Camp 2026
.
Join Richard for
Conwy parkrun
,
which is the closest parkrun to where we will be staying.
Course Description:
The parkrun follows a simple point-to-point route along the banks of the river Conwy, with two short diversions towards Conwy castle on the outward and return section of the event. It is mostly flat, only climbing when it crosses the railway line over a pedestrian bridge. The path used for the parkrun is gravel/tarmac throughout.
About parkrun
The course is 5000m (5km) long. This has been accurately measured by parkrun with a professional measuring wheel. You run/walk at your own pace, don't be scared that you may be slower than other people. Some walk, some run, some jog, some do a combination of all three.
To take part in a parkrun and get your official time,
you will need to have
registered
to receive your all important barcode
.
You will need to bring this barcode with you either printed or on your phone.
Parkrun events are run nationwide and indeed internationally, so once you're signed up you can attend anywhere.  If you don't have a barcode you can still run but won't get a time.
Meeting point:
It is an early start (
8:00 meet on site for 8:10 departure to drive the 22mins
. The event takes place at Conwy Cob, Llandudno Junction, Conwy, LL32 8UB, so please be prompt - we will not be able to wait for latecomers.
If you are coming along to parkrun and wish to do a walk after you will need to sign up for a walk that starts after 10:00 as you will be hard-pushed to get back and ready for any event leaving at 10.00am or earlier. You will need to drive to the start, and where possible car share.
Please see under Location for meeting plans.
The full parkrun event link is here:
https://www.parkrun.org.uk/conwy/
Dogs
parkrun allow one dog per person on a short lead (not extending).  Running with more than one dog, extending leads or waist leads are not permitted at parkrun events.  As a third party event, OutdoorLads does not have any control over this.
OutdoorLads Running Groups
If you want to join the Outdoorlads' national group on the parkrun website, on your parkrun account select Profile, then groups and type "Outdoorlads (UK)" and click the Join button.
You may also wish to sign up to the
Strava OutdoorLads running club:
https://www.strava.com/clubs/439735
Photo credits - Creative Commons License - Chris Morgan, Arek Adeoye, and Huckster - Unsplash; Colin Renshaw and W Allaway and used with permission
Difficulty & Distance: The course is run on a mixture of tarmac paths and gravel paths.

Title: BSC26: Via Ferrata & Abseiling (Sun PM)
Summary:
This is one of the events organised as part of
BIG Spring Camp 2026
.
Embark on an unforgettable adventure with our trio of exhilarating activities. Experience the unique thrill of Via Ferrata, a historic "iron way" that winds its way along a dramatic rock face. Feel the rush as you descend on an epic abseil, taking in the stunning scenery. It's the perfect blend of adventure, teamwork, and breathtaking views.
This event will be led by a professional climbing instructor.
Photos provided by A Day's Adventure and OutdoorLads Members
Difficulty & Distance: This event is suitable for those new to these activities

Title: BSC26: Aero Explorer (Sun PM)
Summary:
Please complete this waiver prior to the event.
This is one of the events organised as part of
BIG Spring Camp 2026
.
A unique adrenaline-rush like no other, Aero Explorer will see you flying at speed along an overhead track, with nothing but the wind below you!
Aero Explorer will take you on a high-speed journey across the quarry like never before. Feel the anticipation building as you climb the two spiral towers before launching off and gliding across 400m of track. As you go, you'll soar above the rugged terrain, taking in breathtaking views of the quarry landscape. Whether you're an experienced thrill-seeker or a first-time adventurer, this new experience will surely leave you feeling exhilarated and energised.
HOW MANY RUNS?
2
FULL EXPERIENCE DURATION
45 minutes
SPEED
Up to 30 mph
DISTANCE
Conquer a huge 400m overhead track. Brace yourself as you dart along three loops.
Restrictions
Weight: Minimum 30kg, Maximum 110kg
Height: Minimum 1.25m, maximum 1.93m

any medical conditions
, please see
check here
to ensure you're eligible to attend this event. You can contact

prior to attending if you have any concerns.
Photos provided by and used with permission from Zip World.

Title: BSC26: Paddleboarding & Canoeing Full day (Sun)
Summary:
This is one of the events organised as part of
BIG Spring Camp 2026
.
Get ready for a fun day of water activities on a stunning lake, hopefully in the sunshine! A whole day of messing around on the water - our qualified instructor will have both stand-up paddleboards (SUP's) and canoes available for you to use on the water, so you can get to experience both in this fun and engaging day on the water!
Learn skills, tackle thrilling challenges, and have chill time on the water too. Get ready to test your teamwork and navigate the waters!
This event will be led by a professional canoeing instructor.
Photos provided by A Day's Adventure and OutdoorLads Members

Title: BSC26: Skyride and Fforest Coaster (Sat PM)
Summary:
Please complete this waiver prior to the event.
This is one of the events organised as part of
BIG Spring Camp 2026
.
Fancy a exhilarating ride on Europe's highest giant swing? Followed by a rollercoaster through the treetops? This is for you!
The Giant Swing
Skyride 2
is Europe's largest giant swing, set in a stunning location overlooking the Conwy Valley. You'll take turns, 5 at a time, to be winched back to a near-vertical position. One of you brave souls will have responsibility of pulling the release string and sending you all hurtling back towards the ground, before rising up and out towards the valley beyond. Completely gravity driven, you'll experience an incredible sense of speed and weightlessness.
HOW MANY?
up to 5 people
HOW HIGH?
24m
DURATION?
15 minutes
Restrictions
Weight: Max. 130kg
Height: Min. 1.2m
The Fforest Coaster
One kilometer of gravity driven twists, turns and thrills through the Fforest wonderland. The Coaster is a twisting barrel of laughs through the trees!
HOW FAR?
365m up & 710m down
HOW FAST?
Up to 25mph
HOW STEEP?
Max 25% gradient
HOW MANY?
3 rides
DURATION?
Approximately 6 mins per run

Title: BSC26: Gorge Scrambling (Sat PM)
Summary:
This is one of the events organised as part of
BIG Spring Camp 2026
.
Challenge yourself on a fun team day out where you'll be jumping into plunge pools, abseiling down waterfalls and generally getting soaked! Gorge walking is loads of fun and we've teamed up with a local supplier of the activity in one of the best parts of Wales to enjoy it. Gorge walking is essentially clambouring up a stream through a narrow gorge, as it flows over boulders and into pools.
You will end up completely wet on this event as you'll be sliding down natural water chutes, jumping into deep pools, scrambling up rocky steps and crawling through holes. Some of the gorges are barely deeper than wellington depth, others have pools that seem bottomless! Be prepared to have a lot of laughs and get very wet!
Photos provided by OutdoorLads Members and A Day's Adventure.

Title: BSC26: Go Below Ultimate Xtreme (Sat)
Summary:
This is one of the events organised as part of
BIG Spring Camp 2026
.
This isn't your average day out—this is a private, OutdoorLads-exclusive underground odyssey. Imagine mine exploration dialed up to eleven: ziplines, bridges, and via ferrata, all set within the heart of our industrial heritage.
The
Ultimate Xtreme™
route runs long and deep beneath the mountains of North Wales. Your journey includes:
9 underground zip lines
, including the world’s longest (130m) and the world's deepest.
Massive depth:
You’ll be navigating tunnels and caverns more than 1,300 feet down. To put that in perspective, the rock roof above you is 1,230 feet thick—the deepest accessible point in Great Britain.
High-octane obstacles:
Tackle high traverses, via ferrata, and ancient bridges.
Duration:
7 hours
Suitability, Weight & Fitness
This seven-hour route is demanding. You don’t need prior experience, but expect to be on the move all day. You’ll be climbing, scrambling, and navigating obstacles that will push your limits. The vertical drops are frequent and will test your nerve, even if you’re usually comfortable with heights.
The Essentials:
Weight:
There is no overall weight limit; however, the "Free Fall" section has a maximum capacity of 19 stone. If you exceed this, you can simply take an alternative path for that segment.
Safety:
All participants must be able to fit securely into a standard safety harness.
Fitness:
You are the best judge of your own physical level. If you’re up for a full day of scrambling, walking, and swinging on ropes, you’re ready for this.
Certain medical conditions may unfortunately impact your trip. So, if you have any of the following please
contact us
(or
Go Below directly
) before booking and you may still be able to be accommodated: Epilepsy, vertigo, mobility issues, issues holding your own weight on arms or legs, amputations or limb function disabilities, deafness or sight impediment and heart problems. They are experienced in offering inclusive trips, so please don't automatically rule yourself out if you have any of the above.
First four photos kindly provided by OutdoorLads Members. Remaining photos kindly provided by Go Below.
Difficulty & Distance: It' jumping, zip lining, climbing, absailing, all in one, for several hours!

Title: BSC26: Bounce Below (Sat AM) - Great for Newbies!
Summary:
Please complete this waiver prior to the event.
This is one of the events organised as part of
BIG Spring Camp 2026
.
This event is great for those who are new (or new-ish) to OutdoorLads or to BSC and coming along alone - a chance to meet others in a similar position and have fun!
If you are booked onto one of the Newbie camps  - this event is especially for YOU!
Imagine giant trampolines, walkways, slides and tunnels all made from netting. Now picture this suspended in a slate cavern hidden underground with funky lighting and you’ll have the right idea of the fun that lies ahead!
The surreal experience of Bounce Below is unlike anything seen before, huge nets are hung in the old slate cavern at varying levels, linked together by tunnels and slides, where you are free to jump, climb and slide, and release the inner child!
Adults emerge from Bounce Below grinning from ear to ear. It brings out the kid in everyone.
HOW MANY NETS?
6
DURATION
1 hour
If you have
any medical conditions
, please see
check here
to ensure you're eligible to attend this event. You can contact
accessibility@zipworld.co.uk
prior to attending if you have any concerns.
Treat yourself
Book this activity and there is still time to book one of our afternoon activities as well!
Photos provided by and used with permission from Zip World.
Difficulty & Distance: It's just jumping

Title: BSC26: Coasteering (Sat)
Summary:
This is one of the events organised as part of
BIG Spring Camp 2026
.
This
full day
activity is one of the BIG Spring Camp highlights! This is a high adrenaline activity and you
will
get wet!
Coasteering involves exploring the rocky coastline, scrambling, climbing, swimming and jumping into the water. It is a challenging wet activity requiring a high level of teamwork.
We use venues on Anglesey which offer a challenge for beginners upwards. Our instructors will encourage teamwork and responsibility throughout the session. Climbs, swims and jumps are all managed to a level appropriate to the individuals ability.
Those taking part will learn how to move on rock, how to look after each other when challenged, develop an appreciation of the marine environment, develop personal confidence, stretch their comfort zone and get wet!
Photos provided by OutdoorLads Members and A Day's Adventure.
Difficulty & Distance: Jumping off cliffs into water, climbing back out - you'll know you've done it!

Title: BSC26: Ultimate White Water Rafting (Sat AM Session 1)
Summary:
This is one of the events organised as part of
BIG Spring Camp 2026
.
Always a favourite BIG Spring Camp activity, white water rafting at the National White Water Centre means taking on the challenging Afon Tryweryn where you'll be swept through crashing waves and boulder strewn rapids on this fast flowing Welsh mountain river.
This two hour session takes you down the Upper Tryweryn’s grade 3-4 natural, Welsh mountain white water rapids, with enough time to usually fit in 4 exciting and challenging runs of the 1.5km section!
Curious?
Here's a video from BSC15
!
Please note that to attend this event, you must have a weight of under 115kg (18st) and be able to swim at least 25 metr
Photos: From OutdoorLads Members at BIG Spring Camp 2024 & 2025
Difficulty & Distance: A reasonable level of fitness will be required to paddle

Title: Go Ape 🦧
Summary:
Go Ape
Climb, cross and zip through the treetops of Crawley — high ropes thrills in a stunning woodland setting.
Push your limits on their most popular high ropes course, packed with tree-to-tree crossings, Tarzan Swings and epic zip lines.
Experience their boldest adventure in the heart of Tilgate Park. Take on five sections with adrenaline-fueled challenges and high-flying thrills, including suspended bridges, two of our legendary Tarzan Swings and the tough ‘stirrups’ crossing.

Title: BIG20 SE1000 The Ultimate Challenge - Haslemere
Summary:
Be part of
Outdoorlads BIG20
with this monster of a challenge!
Conquer
1000m
of ascent over a
half marathon
length walk 21.7k (13.5 miles).
Exclusive
to Outdoorlads and
a first
in the South East of England.
Be awarded a
champion medal
on completion.
Steep slopes, scrambly banks, and hidden paths through access land: this is a route for stamina, power and resilience! Incredible beauty, through gorgeous scenery, heathland, forests, nature-filled copses and valleys. THis is the make up of
SE1000 The Ultimate Challenge
.
Leaders will coach, give details of Kilometres (K) and ascent (m) covered and how far to go. We'll share tips on handling the terrain and developing muscle: expect to be super-charged all over from this experience!
To enjoy this challenge
you should be ready and have:
Completed an SE500
walk or mountain walk or equivalent, and feel confident about another
Readied you muscles
in the 2 weeks before the walk, such as inclined treadmill or walking slopes
Have good footwear,
worn in and able to take the rough paths in places
Wear appropriate layers
and checked the kit list
Plenty of water and snacks
to keep you going - expect to burn 4100 calories!
The route is awesome, includes one of seven wonders of the south of England and four of the highest hills.
Meeting at
Haslemere railway station
, we first rise up Weydown Road with a 5 minute gentle warm up. Then we descend on a path alongside community fields and cross a stream, before taking an ancient byway through shaded trees to Farnham Lane. At this point, you will have completed 2.4K and 111m of ascent. Warm up over, you are now in for your first steep slopes in
Polecat Valley
.
The valley’s name is fitting because captive Polecats have been returned to the wild here in recent years. The descent is steep and scrambly in wet weather. The rise through scrub is also steep but rewarding, giving you the first taste of greater ascents and descents to come.
The rise up
Hindhead Spur
is steeper, 50 metres in one go, and over stony ground in places. The gravel and rounded stones in the clay rich soil slopes were deposited here in the last but one ice age, giving a primeval feel to the landscape. The heath is a reserve which support reptiles including lizards and natterjack toads, as well as birds such as the woodlark and nightjar and the silver-studded blue butterfly. Bell heather and bilberry also thrive here.
There’s no stopping you, tough neanderthal warriors! By the path junction where the spur levels, you will have completed 4K and 220m of ascent. Climbing up and down through the valley there is now a steep descent followed by a steep rise into Coomb head and then woodlands managed by the forestry commission alongside the bridleway beyond. We rise to
Hurt Hill, 262m
, one of four major tops on this route.
On the descent we pause to look at the Temple of the four Winds. This was a folly built in 1910 by a businessman fascinated by the outlook seen from here. Next, a descent to the Roundles and a rise to the second and highest point of the walk,
Gibbet Hill at 272m
. Highwaymen robbers were executed here for their crimes. There is a Celtic Cross memorial here. The area is also of literary fame: Charles Dickens mentions it in Nicholas Nickleby, as a staging point on his route to London.
We are now above the
Devil’s Punch Bowl
. This is a natural amphitheatre, formed by springs eroding the landscape from underneath. According to legend, the devil tormented Thor, the god of thunder, by jumping from hill to hill here. Thor threw lighting bolts at the devil, who evaded them by scooping up earth to throw at Thor. That scooped up earth left the depression known as the Punch Bowl.
At this stage, if you are feeling worn out, there is a short cut to the visitor centre café. You can have an extended break whilst the group completed the northern section. The short cut reduces the total by 300m and 6K.
The north part continues with a steep descent into the Punch Bowl, then a rise to the third main hill of the day,
Highcombe Hill 236m
. The scenery is wild and this is a Site of Special Scientific Interest, home to the rare lesser spotted woodpecker, common redstart and in summer, the wood warbler. Going over two ridges, the route descends to a valley then climbs back to the
café
for a pause for refreshments. This is the top of
Hindhead at 257m
, You have now completed 17.3K and 896m of ascent with 4.2K and 124m to go, so there’s much more downhill now!
Miss James of Tyndall’s wood was a pauper born in 1831 and servant to a family of wine merchants here. When they died without heir, they bequeathed the whole estate to her, and she left it to the National Trust. We walk through her lands and on to
Nutcombe Valley
, which features a bat barn where the pink tinged Natterer’s bats roost.
We continue through
Polecat Copse
which reputedly has the largest redwood trees in Britain. Some have names including King Kong and T rex, and one rises to over 43m high. Nearly there! One last descent and then rise again into Haslemere for a Champion’s completion back at the railway station.
The whole route is 1020m of ascent over a half marathon length 21.7K. Follow the
route on OS Maps
.

Title: BIRMINGHAM Indoor Climbing - MONDAY - Experienced
Summary:
Join OutdoorLads for an evening at Redpoint Birmingham Climbing Centre.
"EXPERIENCED" in this instance is those that have previously climbed & can answer YES to all three of these questions:
Can you fit a climbing harness correctly?
Can you tie in to a rope using the correct knots?
Can you belay another climber correctly & confidently?
Please ensure you only sign up for this event if you
satisfy all three of the prerequisites as an experienced (independent) climber!
Never climbed before? Need a refresher on the basics? Answered NO to any of the three questions above? You will need to
attend one of the beginners (supervised) events
instead, where we will happily sign you in to be supervised (two people per leader maximum), show you the ropes & teach you everything you need to know to climb safely! Beginner (supervised) events are not offered every week.
If you are climbing with us for the first time, whether Experienced or Beginner, it is essential that you send a note to the Event Leader telling them about your previous climbing experience & your goals!
Bouldering
- Most of us do roped climbing, but if you prefer to do bouldering, you can sign yourself in & go bouldering (unsupervised) instead of, or as well as, roped climbing. Please meet the leader & the group at the start though; some others might wish to do bouldering too!
Please note that we will not teach lead climbing or lead belaying on this event -
we would suggest having some
lessons in Lead Climbing
by a qualified member of staff if this is something you're interested in pursuing!
Whilst this event finishes at 8pm, the centre is open until 10pm if you wish to continue climbing for longer!
If you are subsequently unable to attend, please cancel off the event as 'no-shows' are not only rude, but also frustrating for the leader & other attendees who may have travelled over an hour away to climb with you!
Redpoint Birmingham Climbing Centre
Indoor climbing centres offer the challenges of climbing that you'll find on natural rock crags - simply indoors! It's a fun way to stay fit, make new friends & develop your climbing skills.
Climbing
- Unlike Indoor Bouldering there is a certain amount of skill to be mastered, such as tying in knots & belaying a partner, as well as the climbs being higher than in bouldering. If you are new to climbing or would like a refresher, then this is just for you!! If you’ve climbed before, can safely tie into a harness & can competently belay, then there’s plenty of choices of top-rope & lead climbing on offer here.
Bouldering
- Instead of tackling long high routes, the 'problems' (as they are referred to) are shorter & focus on technique, balance, body positioning & sometimes power. With the different emphasis, bouldering doesn't have the hassle of climbing with ropes, so it’s excellent (but not necessarily easier) for newcomers.
Redpoint Birmingham Climbing Centre
has more than 75 lines with over 300 routes, set through some spectacular ground including its notorious 'stalactite'. It even features one of the few indoor 'Yorkshire VS cracks' in the country, to test your jamming skills. The bouldering rooms are just as interesting as its climbing routes, over 200 bouldering problems have been organised into graded circuits to provide something for everyone.
You MUST register via this event page before coming to the centre!
To climb, you will need to:
Complete a
Registration Form
(please arrive early to do this in-person the first time) & pay a Registration Fee:
Annual Registration
is
£5
or
Day Registration
is
£3
Pay an
Entry Fee
directly to the climbing centre:
Standard Entry
is
£12.50
&
Bouldering Only
is
£8.50.
Hire
climbing shoes (
£3.50
) & a climbing harness (
£3.50
) if you don't own your own.
Please see Redpoint's
website
for current prices & note that prices are subject to change.
If you have any questions or need any more information just get in touch with the event leader by clicking on their profile below, or take a look at the
My first climbing event page
for more information.
PLEASE NOTE:
All those attending the event must also be aware of the climbing centre's own terms & conditions of participation & abide by them, along with any instructions given by the centre's staff.
Photos:
taken & used with permission of
R. Casabuena
&
K. Jones
.
Difficulty & Distance: This event is for EXPERIENCED (independent) climbers only!!!

Title: The Willingham Woods & Viking Way Day Walk
Summary:
Join Chris for a 8.5-mile (13.5 km) walk starting in Willingham Woods, a popular spot just outside Market Rasen known for its peaceful trails, tall pines and mix of broadleaf trees. Managed by Forestry England, the woods are home to deer, woodpeckers and foxes, and provide a calm and shaded start to the walk.
We’ll head through the woodland towards Hamilton Hill, where the trees begin to open up and views across the surrounding countryside start to appear. From here, we make our way to Walesby, a small village best known for the “Ramblers’ Church.” Sitting on the hillside, All Saints’ Old Church is a quiet and atmospheric spot with a stained-glass window depicting walkers and cyclists in honour of its place on the Viking Way.
From Walesby, we follow the Viking Way along the edge of the Lincolnshire Wolds, taking in rolling hills and wide open skies. This is a gentle and scenic stretch with plenty of views as we head towards the village of Tealby.
Often described as one of Lincolnshire’s prettiest villages, Tealby is full of character, with stone cottages, winding lanes and over 30 listed buildings. In 1807, the famous Tealby Hoard was discovered here, containing more than 5,000 silver coins from the time of Henry II. We’ll stop at The King’s Head, a thatched pub dating back to the 14th century and thought to be the oldest in the county. With a cosy interior and a lovely garden, it is the perfect place for a refreshing drink.
After our stop, we begin the final leg of the walk, returning through Willingham Woods along forest paths. For those who fancy a post-walk drink or bite to eat, the Willingham Woods Bike Café is right by the car park and a great way to finish the day.
For those interested in a Parkrun before the event, the nearest one is in
Market Rasen
, just 2.3 miles (5 minutes) away.
Your First OutdoorLads Event?
We know that new members in particular like to know in advance more about what an OutdoorLads event will be like.
If this is your first OutdoorLads event, you should check out the information designed specifically for you on the
My First Event
page on the OutdoorLads website.
What if there is a Waiting List?
If this event is fully booked and you want to attend, just add yourself to the waitlist, because places inevitably become available closer to the date.
Want to keep in touch with what's going on in the Midlands / ODL Central Region?
Sign up for the
Central Region Telegram Chat Group
, and bookmark our upcoming events list via the short link:
https://outdoorlads.events/central
.
Image Credits:
All images taken by the event leader with permission for ODL use.
Difficulty & Distance: A 8.5-miles (13.5km) walk. A 510ft (155m) ascent.

Title: Round Dunsop Bridge on a Thursday
Summary:
Join Stephen on an
11 mile
walk in and around
Dunsop Bridge
in
Lancashire
in the heart of the Forest of Bowland and in the centre of Great Britain.
We will start by following the River Hodder for a while then climb up to onto the moors overlooking the Hodder Valley.
We will venture through many farms and walk along the tops with views across to the Bowland Fells then returning back to Dunsop Bridge.
When we arrive back in the village -let's find the Centre of Great Britain marker!
There is no pub in the village  - but we can take a short drive to the wonderful Inn at Whitewell for some post walk drinks - here is a link -
http://www.innatwhitewell.com/
Image / photo credits:  All leader's own images - with permission for OutdoorLads to use and reuse.
Are you new to Outdoorlads and considering your first event?
Have a look at this link, hopefully it will help.
https://www.outdoorlads.com/my-first-event
Don’t worry if the event is full.
Do sign up and join the waiting list.
Often those on the main list cancel nearer the time.
There is a very high chance you will still get a place
Difficulty & Distance: There is slow ascent up onto the fells

Title: Berwick to Alfriston Circular Leisure Walk
Summary:
Join us for a relaxed and social countryside walk through the heart of the South Downs
🚶 Walk Overview
A popular
8.5‑mile circular leisure walk
starting and finishing at
Berwick Railway Station
. The route begins on the scenic
Cuckmere Pilgrim Path
, following the river valley and open countryside with stunning panoramic views.
The walk is
generally easy to moderate
, mostly flat with a few gentle inclines and short sections of road walking. Some paths can be muddy after rain.
⭐ Route Highlights
🌄
Cuckmere Valley
– wide open views and peaceful paths
🗿
The Long Man of Wilmington
– one of Sussex’s most iconic landmarks
🍺
Long Man Brewery
– award‑winning local ales brewed on site, with a relaxed taproom and countryside views; a great spot for lunch and an optional pint or tasting or visit to the café next door
🏘
Alfriston Village
– charming historic high street
The
Star Inn
(dating back to 1345)
The
Clergy House
(England’s first National Trust property)
You’ll have around
45 minutes free time in Alfriston
to explore, relax, or grab a slice of cake or ice-cream.
📏 Walk Details
Distance:
Approx. 8.5 miles
Difficulty:
Easy / Moderate
Terrain:
Largely flat, some gentle climbs, possible muddy sections
Pace:
Leisurely and social
🚆 Travel Information
Start / Finish:
Berwick Railway Station (East Sussex)
By Train:
Easily accessible from
Brighton, Lewes, and Eastbourne
By Car:
Parking available at the station or near Berwick Station
By Bus (Optional Return):
It is possible to return by
local Cuckmere Buses
from
Alfriston
back towards
Berwick or Seaford
.
Services are limited and
run on specific days only
, so please
check routes and timings in advance
.
👉
Check up‑to‑date timetables:
https://www.cuckmerebuses.org.uk/timetables.htm
Common routes that may be useful include services
126
and
47 (Cuckmere Valley Ramblerbus)
, subject to day and seasonal operation.
New Members
If this is your first flight or you are new to OutdoorLads passenger we’d love to see you. This type of event is perfect for you to get to know other ODL members. Please message me if you have any questions.
Photo credits martiebear free for outdoorlads to use
Difficulty & Distance: 8.5 mile  generally easy to moderate
